projectDescription: An open-source all-sky camera system for Raspberry Pi that captures, processes, and publishes night-sky images with features like keograms, startrails, timelapse videos, and customizable image overlays.
keyFeatures: Automated night-sky image capture using ZWO and Raspberry Pi cameras
Web User Interface (WebUI) for administration, live image viewing, and configuration
Modular post-processing pipeline: overlays, star counting, meteor detection, weather data
Keogram generation — a timeline image stitched from nightly captures
Startrails — stacked composite images showing star movement across the night
Automatic timelapse video creation at end of each night
Remote website publishing via FTP/SCP for sharing images publicly
Dark frame subtraction to remove hot pixels from images
Drag-and-drop Overlay Editor for text, images, and data fields
Allsky Map integration to share camera location globally
publicApiSurface: allsky.sh — main entry point to start the allsky capture service
install.sh — full installation script for Raspberry Pi
upgrade.sh — in-place upgrade from a prior version
scripts/saveImage.sh — saves and processes each captured image
scripts/modules/allsky_overlay.py — overlay module for annotating images
scripts/modules/allsky_starcount.py — module to count stars in an image
scripts/modules/allsky_export.py — module to export images externally
scripts/timelapse.sh — generate timelapse video from image set
scripts/upload.sh — upload images/videos to remote servers
